domain_coverage_comments: Entities: drug,target,disease via UMLS CUIs; Relations: TREATS, INHIBITS, ASSOCIATED_WITH (~30 predicate types). Rich coverage but many edges generic/ambiguous.
source_scope_score_comment: Broad, cross-disease; >21M PubMed citations; 57.6M predications; thousands of drugs, diseases, genes; multi-relation subtypes
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: TREATS, INHIBITS, CAUSES, etc. present but NLP-extracted, noisy; predicates often generic; PubMed links but no confidence scores; precision ~70-80% → heavy filtering required
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Predications are NLP-extracted, dominated by generic predicates like “ASSOCIATED_WITH.” Precision reported 70-80% depending on domain; recall lower. High noise without filtering. Needs strong confidence/frequency guardrails.

infores_name: Search Tool for the Retrieval of Interacting Genes/Proteins
domain_coverage_comments: Primarily single-entity (targets) but exceptionally rich in intra-entity associations and descriptors - borderline between 1 and 2. - Scoring a 2 to reward the rich intra-entity relations
source_scope_score_comment: Global-scale, multi-species PPI/functional knowledgebase with millions of high-confidence edges from heterogeneous evidence.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Directly usable as a backbone for repurposing pipelines; confidence-scored PPI edges integrate seamlessly once drug and disease mappings are added.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: STRING curated edges and scored predictions are confidence-weighted (experiment, database, co-expression, text mining). Noise is mitigated by star/confidence scores; making it not penalty-worthy.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: drugs (40,947 small molecules), proteins/enzymes (9,625 from UniProt), metabolites, diseases; Relations: metabolic reactions, drug-protein interactions, protein-protein interactions (signaling), transport; Xrefs: extensive (HMDB, DrugBank IDs, UniProt IDs present). However, limited T-D edges; pathways focus on drug action/metabolism and disease mechanisms but don't provide systematic D-D or T-D associations.
source_scope_score_comment: Broad, cross-disease (metabolic, signaling, disease pathways); Multi-species (Human, Mouse, etc.); ~4,000 drugs, ~20,000 proteins, >100,000 pathways (including computational expansions in PathBank)
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct mechanistic edges: Drug-Target (inhibits/activates/modulates) explicitly modeled in "Drug Action Pathways"; Pathway-Disease associations are strong. Plug-and-play for mechanistic inference
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Noise 0: Predominantly manually curated (gold-standard Wishart lab curation). High precision

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug, target, disease; Relations: D-T, T-D, D-D/indication, AE; xrefs common to UniProt, PubChem, MeSH, ChEMBL; descriptors rich with synonyms and identifiers supporting multi-hop traversal
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-disease and multi-species coverage; thousands of drugs and targets; millions of edges spanning small molecules, biologics, and metabolites; multiple relation subtypes including D-T, D-D, indication, interaction
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-T, T-D, D-D/AE clearly defined; direction and effect explicit (inhibitor, agonist, substrate); evidence and confidence standard; RxNorm, HGNC, UniProt mappings clean
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated manual database with high precision ≥85%; mechanistic and pharmacologic assertions verified; minimal generic or text-mined noise

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: Comprehensive coverage of all OBO domains: Chemical (CHEBI), Disease (MONDO), Phenotype (HP), Anatomy (UBERON), Gene/Protein (PRO/GO), Function (GO). Relations: Rich logical definitions (is_a, part_of, regulates, has_phenotype) pre-computed via OWL reasoning
source_scope_score_comment: Broad & Massive: It is the backbone of the biomedical semantic web, integrating ~40+ ontologies. Millions of classes and edges. Covers all species (GO/Uberon) and all disease areas (MONDO)
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect / Scaffold: While it provides the essential vocabulary and hierarchy (A is a B; X is part of Y), it lacks direct, experimental "Drug-Treats-Disease" or "Drug-Binds-Target" edges derived from clinical trials or assays. It is used to ground data and calculate semantic similarity (e.g., via phenotypes), not to lookup treatments directly
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Noise 0: Composed of manually curated OBO ontologies. High precision and logical consistency

domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: genes only; no D-T/T-D/D-D or AE edges; relations limited to genomic features, transcripts, and identifiers; xrefs to HGNC, UniProt, and NCBI Gene present but no pharmacologic or disease relations
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-species genomic coverage including human and multiple model organisms; millions of gene entries and annotations; one main relation family (genomic hierarchy)
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no mechanistic or therapeutic edges; data focused on gene structure, variants, and coordinates; not usable for D→D modeling without integration
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated manual and computational database with high precision ≥85%; no text-mined or ambiguous associations

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug, target, disease; Relations: D-T and T-D with quantitative binding affinities (Ki, IC50, EC50); xrefs common to UniProt, PubChem, and ChEMBL; descriptors rich supporting D→T→D traversal
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-species and cross-disease coverage; millions of experimentally measured binding interactions; thousands of proteins and small molecules; multiple interaction subtypes across modalities
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-T/T-D with explicit effect type and quantitative strength; direction clear; confidence high with curated experimental assays; mappings to UniProt and PubChem clean and complete
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated manual-experimental dataset with high precision ≥85%; assay-level confidence; minimal or no generic associations

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: target,gene + disease; Relation: T-D (~millions); mapped to DOID, STRING/HGNC/Ensembl; confidence scoring robust. No drugs or D-D/AEs.
source_scope_score_comment: Broad, cross-disease; millions of T-D edges; >19k genes, >8k diseases; integrates curated, GWAS, text mining; updated weekly.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: T-D; millions of associations; 1-5 star confidence; AUC=0.916 for text mining. No direct drug links, no mechanism or AE context → requires integration with drug-target DBs.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Text-mined associations make up the majority of edges (~4.5M vs. 36k curated). ROC AUC is high (0.916 for text mining), but still substantial “gene associated with disease” edges that are correlative and noisy. Can be rescued with 4-5 star confidence. Still only moderate penalty as eventhough DISEASES has lots of text-mined noise, but also curated channels, GWAS, and explicit confidence bins. Because the noisy part can be rescued cleanly (4-5 stars), we treat it as -1 (moderate noise), not -2.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: organisms only (bacteria, viruses, fungi, plants, animals, protozoans); Relations: hierarchical taxonomic classifications (is_a/subclass relationships through kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, species); no genes, proteins, drugs, diseases, or phenotypes. No D-T, T-D, D-D, or biomedical relation families; purely taxonomic lineage data. IDs: TaxID standard identifiers with synonyms/common names present.
source_scope_score_comment: Broad, comprehensive: >460,000 formal species names; >1.34M additional species-level nodes; total taxonomy tree with hierarchical structure spanning all domains of life (bacteria, archaea, eukaryota, viruses); multi-organism across all kingdoms; international standard (INSDC); manually curated; serves as classification backbone for GenBank/EMBL/DDBJ; extensive synonymy tracking; covers human, model organisms, pathogens, environmental species. Truly global taxonomic scope.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect: provides organism context only - no drug, target, or disease data. Useful for annotating which organism a gene/protein/target comes from (e.g., "this protein is from Homo sapiens TaxID:9606" or "this pathogen is Plasmodium falciparum TaxID:5833"), enabling species-specific queries and filtering (e.g., "find human targets," "exclude bacterial proteins"). Critical for cross-species comparative analysis (identifying orthologous targets across species for model organism studies). However, contains zero D-T, T-D, or D-D edges. No pharmacological predicates. Cannot generate repurposing candidates without integration with pharmacological sources. Clean TaxID mapping facilitates joining with other databases (UniProt, Entrez Gene). Best used as organism disambiguation/normalization layer.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Manually curated by NCBI taxonomy specialists; follows international nomenclature codes (ICZN, ICNafp, ICNP, ICVCN); precision ≥85% for taxonomic assignments; authoritative INSDC standard; extensive literature-based validation; clean hierarchical structure; no text-mining or predictions; curators resolve nomenclatural conflicts systematically.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: metabolite, gene protein, disease; Relations: metabolite-gene protein and metabolite-disease associations present; xrefs to KEGG, ChEBI, UniProt, PubChem observed; drug entities limited and indirect
source_scope_score_comment: Multi disease human focused coverage; thousands of metabolites and conditions; moderate scale with biochemical emphasis; single modality centered on metabolites
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only for repurposing; lacks direct D-T, D-D indication, or AE edges; relations largely biochemical or associative without pharmacologic direction or clinical indication context
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to many generic metabolite-disease associations without causal direction; precision varies across entries; filtering needed to retain mechanistically relevant links

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: targets and diseases; Relation: T-D consistently present across many records; includes PMIDs and evidence strings; xrefs to UniProt and EFO observed
source_scope_score_comment: Multi-disease human coverage at mid-scale; thousands of targets and diseases; tens of thousands of edges; captures a wide therapeutic spectrum but not a full multi-modality backbone
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: T-D associations mined from literature; PMIDs present for most; direction/effect partial and context often correlative rather than mechanistic; mappings to UniProt and EFO reasonably aligned
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to text-mined associations with variable precision (~70-85%); many edges labeled “associated with” without mechanistic clarity; confidence scoring helps filter high-quality subset

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene protein; Relations: T-T protein-protein interactions consistently present; xrefs to UniProt and other protein databases observed; no drug or disease entities present
source_scope_score_comment: Multi organism PPI resource with tens to hundreds of thousands of interactions; human and model species covered; single modality focused on molecular interactions
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only for repurposing; no D-T, T-D, D-D, indication, or AE edges; PPIs provide mechanistic context but require downstream integration for drug-disease modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated and experimentally supported interactions with confidence annotations; precision ≥85%; minimal generic noise

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: disease, drug, gene, anatomy, procedure; Relations: disease classification, drug-disease therapeutic use, gene-disease associations present; xrefs common to UMLS, MeSH, SNOMED CT, HGNC; relations largely terminological or definitional
source_scope_score_comment: Cancer-centric focus (domain-specific, not broad cross-disease); 40,000+ cancer/neoplasm concepts with granular classification; 26,000 drugs/agents including comprehensive combination chemotherapy regimens; human-focused; mid-scale: hundreds of thousands of concepts but narrow therapeutic scope vs. pan-disease resources; covers clinical care, translational research, basic science; Federal CHI standard anatomy; strong in oncology pharmacology but limited outside cancer domain.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only for repurposing; therapeutic use relations present but lack direction effect strength or evidence granularity; no explicit D-T binding or AE edges usable without heavy curation
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Manually curated by NCI Enterprise Vocabulary Services (EVS); description logic modeling with automated consistency checks; monthly updates (~700 new concepts/release); extensive QA lifecycle including automated checks, manual review, external partner evaluation; Federal CHI standard; precision ≥85% for curated concepts; concept history tracked; used in production NCI systems (cancer.gov, caCORE, clinical trials coding).

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene, anatomy tissue, developmental stage; Relations: gene-expression-anatomy consistently present; xrefs to Ensembl, Uberon, NCBI Gene observed; no drug entities and no disease entities as primary objects
source_scope_score_comment: Multi species gene expression resource covering human and model organisms; tens of thousands of genes across many tissues and stages; single modality focused on expression
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only for repurposing; no D-T, T-D, D-D, indication, or AE edges; expression context useful for mechanism interpretation but not directly usable for D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated and integrated expression calls with quality filtering; precision ≥85%; no generic text mined associations

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug, target gene, disease, pathway, anatomy, adverse effect; Relations: D-T, T-D, D-D indication, AE, pathway membership, PPI present; xrefs to DrugBank, ChEMBL, UniProt, DOID, MeSH observed
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross disease general purpose KG; integrates many upstream sources; hundreds of thousands of edges across drugs, genes, diseases, pathways, and AEs; multi modality backbone
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Multiple direct edge families are strong (D-T + T-D, or D-D + AE). Direction/effect is clear for most edges AND evidence/confidence is routine. Negatives / clinical context exist AND IDs align cleanly; meaning plug-and-play for repurposing, not just enrichment
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: core D-T, T-D, and D-D edges are manually curated from trusted databases by Daniel Himmelstein et al. at UCSF/Penn; gold-standard indications validated; documented QC, de-duplication, and standardized vocabularies; benchmarked precision ≥85%

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: disease, drug chemical, anatomy, phenotype; relations mainly hierarchical classification; descriptors rich with synonyms and identifiers and xrefs via UMLS
source_scope_score_comment: Broad general purpose biomedical thesaurus spanning most diseases, drugs, anatomy, and concepts at large scale
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no clear D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges for modeling; relations are terminological and hierarchical without direction or clinical context
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated vocabulary with high precision and no text mined unscored assertions

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene protein, protein family, pathway, GO function; Relations: gene-family membership and gene-pathway associations consistently present; xrefs to UniProt, GO, and Ensembl observed; no drug or disease entities
source_scope_score_comment: Multi organism functional classification covering many protein families and pathways; thousands of genes across species; moderate scale focused on functional annotation
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; functional and pathway context useful for mechanism interpretation but not directly usable for D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated functional classification with expert review; high precision and no text mined generic assertions

infores_name: Library of Integrated Network-Based Cellular Signatures
domain_coverage_comments: Entities: drugs + genes (genetic perturbations), disease mainly as context; relations are perturbation→signature (L1000, P100/GCP, imaging). Rich metadata/FAIR, but no native D-T/T-D/D-D edges
source_scope_score_comment: Broad, cross-disease & multi-modality (L1000, proteomics, imaging, epigenetics); tens of thousands of perturbagens; hundreds of thousands of signatures; large multi-center program; FAIR & standardized.”
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Strong for signature-based D→D (reversal/mimic) via CLUE/L1000CDS2; toxicity signatures (DToxS) aid filtering. Lacks curated D-T/T-D/D-D edges; needs join to DrugBank/ChEMBL/BindingDB and disease resources.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Perturbation signatures are reproducible and high-quality, but noise arises from cell-type/context-specific variability. Not text-mining noise, but biological variability → requires guardrails (dose/time/cell filtering). Moderate penalty.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene protein, pathway, reaction, complex, small molecule; Relations: pathway membership, reaction participation, causal process links present; xrefs to UniProt, ChEBI, GO, Ensembl common; disease and drug entities present but secondary and not consistently linked
source_scope_score_comment: Broad general purpose pathway knowledgebase covering many diseases and biological processes; human focused with orthology projections; hundreds of thousands of reactions and pathway links; backbone scale
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges exist via pathway annotated drug participation and disease pathways but D-T and D-D indication edges are indirect and inferred through pathways; direction and mechanistic causality present but not framed as therapeutic assertions; usable with guardrails
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: expert curated pathways with detailed mechanistic assertions and high precision ≥85%

domain_coverage_comments: Covers diseases, drugs/chemicals, genes/genomes, anatomy, phenotypes, procedures, functions; concepts standardized to CUIs with 134 semantic types and 54 relation families.
source_scope_score_comment: Unifies ~60 vocabularies (~900k concepts) spanning clinical medicine and life sciences, from MeSH and SNOMED to NCBI Taxonomy and early GO integration.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Provides schema-level relations (e.g., ‘Pharmacologic Substance TREATS Disease or Syndrome’, ‘interacts with’) but not instance-level curated edges, so mainly useful for normalization and relation templates.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Very broad vocabulary integration, but a large portion of concepts/relations are non-actionable for D-T-D repurposing (billing codes, administrative vocabularies, generic ontology terms). Useful backbone, but includes a lot of 'non-repurposing noise'.

domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: Protein only including isoforms and modified forms; relations limited to protein hierarchy and modification states; no drug or disease entities; no D-T/T-D/D-D relations
source_scope_score_comment: Multi organism coverage of protein forms and modifications; thousands of proteins and isoforms; moderate scale focused on protein-centric representation
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; relations describe protein forms and states without pharmacologic or disease linkage
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated ontology with expert review; high precision and no text mined or generic associations

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene target and disease; Relations: T-D gene-disease associations consistently present across records; xrefs to HGNC, UniProt, MeSH, MONDO observed; no drug entities or D-T/D-D relations
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-disease and multi-organism coverage; hundreds of thousands of gene-disease associations; integrates curated sources, GWAS, and literature; large-scale backbone for disease genetics
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: T-D associations used at scale in practice; scores and evidence levels provided; direction and effect largely correlative rather than mechanistic; mappings to HGNC and disease ontologies generally usable
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to mix of curated and text-mined associations; many edges generic “associated with”; confidence scores enable filtering to higher-quality subsets

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene and disease; Relation: T-D consistently present across records; xrefs to HGNC, NCBI Gene, and MONDO; descriptors detailed with synonyms and inheritance patterns
source_scope_score_comment: Multi-disease human coverage focused on genetic and rare disorders; thousands of gene-disease entries; moderate breadth across many phenotypes but limited to hereditary mechanisms
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no direct D-T or D-D edges; provides mechanistic T-D context but lacks pharmacologic, direction, or effect terms; valuable for disease mechanism mapping but not direct repurposing
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated manual source with high precision ≥85%; all entries expert-reviewed; no text-mined or generic associations

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: targets/proteins, ligands/compounds, diseases; Relations: D-T, T-D, D-D target-mediated via DrugCentral integration; Descriptors: TDL classification, TIN-X scores, tissue expression, structures, pathways; Cross-refs: UniProt, ChEMBL, DrugBank, OMIM, Mondo, GO.
source_scope_score_comment: Broad target centric platform covering thousands of human proteins across druggability classes Tclin Tchem Tbio Tdark; integrates multiple upstream databases; large scale cross disease scope
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-T and T-D available through aggregation; evidence and provenance tracked by source; direction and effect inconsistent across inputs; requires source level filtering to be modeling ready
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to heterogeneous integration and inherited associations from multiple sources; precision varies by target class and upstream database

domain_coverage_comments: Rich ontology of small molecules with roles and structure descriptors; cross-referenced broadly; but lacks explicit gene/disease edges.
source_scope_score_comment: General-purpose backbone ontology of small molecules, roles, and classes; >400k entities; multi-resource integration; cross-disease, multi-use.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Great for harmonization (IDs, synonyms, chemical roles), but lacks curated D-T, T-D, or D-D edges; not plug-and-play for repurposing.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Fully curated ontology; stable IDs and cross-refs. No evidence of systematic noise.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: phenotype and disease; Relations: disease-phenotype annotations and hierarchical phenotype structure present; xrefs to OMIM, Orphanet, MONDO common; no drug or target entities
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-disease phenotype backbone covering thousands of diseases and phenotypes; human-focused but widely used across rare and common disease contexts; large-scale ontology
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; phenotype associations useful for disease characterization but not directly usable for D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated expert-maintained ontology with high precision; relations are deterministic and non-noisy

domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: gene protein only; relations link genes to GO terms (biological process, molecular function, cellular component); no drug or disease entities; no D-T, T-D, or D-D relations; xrefs to UniProt common
source_scope_score_comment: Broad multi species functional annotation backbone; millions of annotations across thousands of organisms; large scale and general purpose
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; annotations describe function and location without therapeutic or disease linkage; not usable for D→D modeling without downstream integration
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated and evidence coded annotations with high precision; no text mined or generic associative noise

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug and adverse event phenotype; Relations: D-D adverse event reports present at scale; xrefs to RxNorm and MedDRA typically available; no targets or diseases as primary modeled entities
source_scope_score_comment: Very large scale postmarketing safety database; millions of reports across thousands of drugs and adverse events; human focused and broad across therapeutic areas
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-D adverse event associations used widely in practice; direction clear drug causes AE; reporting context present; high reporting bias and confounding require filtering and disproportionality methods
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to spontaneous reporting bias underreporting duplication and confounding by indication; signal detection requires statistical guardrails

infores_name: Foundational Model of Anatomy Ontology (FMA -- both from UMLS and from OBO)
domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: anatomical structure only; relations limited to anatomical hierarchy and part_of relations; no drug, target, or disease entities; xrefs to UMLS and OBO identifiers present
source_scope_score_comment: 75,000+ anatomical classes covering canonical human anatomy; integrated into UMLS and OBO Foundry; reference ontology for biomedical informatics; used in medical imaging, EHRs, education-anatomical vocabulary backbone but domain-specific (anatomy only)
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Zero pharmacological content: FMA describes anatomical structure (heart, liver, neurons) without drugs, targets, diseases, or any pharmacological/clinical relationships-anatomical reference vocabulary only, no repurposing edges.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated expert-built ontology with deterministic anatomical relations and high precision

infores_name: National Center for Biotechnology Information Gene
domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: gene only; relations limited to gene identifiers, aliases, genomic context, and links to external resources; no D-T, T-D, D-D, or AE relations inside the source
source_scope_score_comment: Broad multi-species gene catalog covering millions of genes across organisms; backbone-scale reference used widely across biomedical databases
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no direct therapeutic or disease relations; provides identifiers and links but requires downstream sources for modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated reference database with high precision and no text-mined generic assertions

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: GO terms only (biological process, molecular function, cellular component); relations limited to hierarchical and part_of relations among GO terms; no drug, gene, or disease entities inside the source; xrefs mainly ontology identifiers
source_scope_score_comment: Broad multi-species ontology backbone covering most biological processes and functions at large scale; widely used across biomedical resources
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; provides functional vocabulary but no therapeutic or disease assertions for modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated expert-maintained ontology with deterministic relations and high precision

domain_coverage_comments: Entity seen: medical procedure only; relations limited to hierarchical and compositional code structure; no drug, target, or disease entities; xrefs primarily within UMLS
source_scope_score_comment: Broad procedural classification covering all inpatient procedures across medical and surgical domains; large scale administrative backbone used nationwide
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; procedural codes do not support therapeutic inference or D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated administrative coding system with deterministic structure and high precision

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: gene protein and tissue anatomy; Relations: gene-tissue expression associations present; xrefs to gene identifiers and anatomy terms observed; no drug or disease entities
source_scope_score_comment: Multi tissue human focused expression resource; thousands of genes across many tissues; moderate scale focused on expression modality
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; expression data provide contextual relevance but not direct therapeutic modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated expression measurements and annotations; high precision and no text mined generic assertions

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug, target, disease; Relations: D-T, T-D, and D-D (chemical-disease) consistently present; xrefs rich across MeSH, NCBI Gene, and PubChem; descriptors well populated enabling D→T→D traversal
source_scope_score_comment: Broad cross-disease and multi-organism coverage; hundreds of thousands of chemical-gene-disease interactions; includes environmental and pharmacologic agents; multiple relation subtypes across species and modalities
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-T, T-D, and D-D/chemical-disease curated and inferred; PMIDs and evidence codes present; direction partial with many “associated with” or “affects” edges; mappings to MeSH and NCBI Gene consistent
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Moderate noise due to mix of curated and inferred/text-mined edges; precision ~70-85%; confidence scores available for filtering; associations partly correlative rather than mechanistic

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug and adverse event; Relation: D-D (AE) consistently present across many records; xrefs to RxNorm, MedDRA, and UMLS; descriptors usable though limited to AE context
source_scope_score_comment: Multi-disease human coverage with thousands of drugs and adverse events; moderate breadth focused on marketed small molecules; limited modality and no target-level or pathway data
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: D-D adverse event pairs curated from package inserts; effect type explicit as AE; direction clear (drug causes AE); mapped cleanly to RxNorm and MedDRA; no mechanistic context beyond clinical effects
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated manual extraction from drug labels; precision ≥85%; high reliability; minimal noise or text-mined ambiguity

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: disease and gene; Relations: T-D gene-disease associations present; disease classification and phenotype links present; xrefs to OMIM, HGNC, ICD, and ORDO observed; no native drug entities
source_scope_score_comment: Rare disease focused resource covering thousands of rare diseases and associated genes; human only; moderate scale but narrow therapeutic scope
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, D-D indication, or AE edges; gene-disease links useful for mechanism understanding but not directly usable for D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated expert maintained rare disease resource with high precision and no text mined generic assertions

domain_coverage_comments: Entities: gene, disease, phenotype; Relations: gene-phenotype (MP), mouse model→human disease (DO/OMIM), gene→GO, gene→expression (GXD); IDs: MGI/DO/MP/NCBI/PMID.
source_scope_score_comment: Backbone mouse resource; cross-disease; many phenotypes/models; multiple relation subtypes (phenotype, expression, GO, disease models); regular releases / bulk access.
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges: T-D (mouse model→human disease) with PMIDs; IDs clean (DO/OMIM/NCBI/MGI). No D-T/D-D/AE—needs drug databases for D→T→D.
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: Curated, literature-backed annotations (phenotypes, disease models, GO, MP, DO). High trust in evidence. No penalty needed.

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug and drug product concepts; Relations: drug-drug equivalence, ingredient, brand-generic, dose form relations present; xrefs to NDC, ATC, SNOMED CT, and UMLS common; no target or disease entities
source_scope_score_comment: Broad general purpose medication terminology covering nearly all US marketed drugs and formulations; large scale backbone used across EHRs and regulatory systems
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Indirect only; no D-T, T-D, D-D indication, or AE edges; supports normalization and mapping but not therapeutic inference or D→D modeling
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated authoritative drug terminology with deterministic relations and high precision

domain_coverage_comments: Entities seen: drug and drug class; relations include drug-class membership, mechanism of action, and physiologic effect; xrefs to RxNorm and UMLS present; disease entities not consistently modeled as first-class relations
source_scope_score_comment: Broad coverage of US medications and therapeutic classes; human focused; moderate scale with limited relation families centered on pharmacologic classification
utility_drugrepurposing_comment: Direct edges exist for drug-mechanism and drug-physiologic effect; limited or indirect disease indication context; no explicit D-T binding or AE modeling; usable with guardrails for class-level inference
noise_penalty_adjustment_comment: No penalty: curated authoritative terminology maintained by NLM and VA; deterministic relations and high precision
